name: expressions
context: HCL expressions — references, interpolation, conditionals, iteration, dynamic blocks, and for_each/count
info: Terraform expressions form the computation and logic layer of HCL. They reference resource attributes, variables, locals, and data sources. String interpolation embeds dynamic values in strings. Conditional expressions choose between values. For expressions transform collections. Splat expressions extract attributes from lists of resources. Dynamic blocks generate repeated nested blocks programmatically. The count and for_each meta-arguments control resource multiplication — count uses an integer index while for_each iterates over maps or sets. Expressions also include operators (arithmetic, comparison, logical), type conversions, and template directives. See [[terraform/hcl/variables]] for the values expressions reference and [[terraform/hcl/functions]] for callable functions within expressions.
triggers: iterate over a list in terraform

sections.title: References
sections.body: Expressions reference values via dot paths: `var.<name>` for input variables, `local.<name>` for locals, `resource_type.name.attribute` for resources, `data.type.name.attribute` for data sources, `module.name.output` for module outputs. Special references: `self` (provisioners), `each.key` / `each.value` (for_each), `count.index` (count), `path.module` / `path.root` / `path.cwd`, `terraform.workspace`. Index into maps with `["key"]` and lists with `[index]`.

sections.title: String interpolation and templates
sections.body: Embed expressions in strings with `${expr}`. Heredoc syntax (`<<-EOT ... EOT`) for multi-line strings with optional indent stripping. Use `templatefile(path, vars)` from [[terraform/hcl/functions]] for external template files supporting `%{ for }` and `%{ if }` directives. Prefer `templatefile()` over inline heredocs for complex user data scripts, IAM policies, and configuration files.

sections.title: Conditional expressions
sections.body: Ternary syntax: `condition ? true_val : false_val`. Both branches must be compatible types. Common patterns: conditional instance sizing (`var.env == "prod" ? "t3.large" : "t3.micro"`), conditional resource creation (`count = var.enabled ? 1 : 0`), and null coalescing with `coalesce()`. Combine with `try()` from [[terraform/hcl/functions]] for safe fallback chains.

sections.title: For expressions and splats
sections.body: For expressions transform collections: `[for item in list : expr]` produces a list, `{for item in list : key => value}` produces a map. Add `if clause` for filtering: `[for s in subnets : s.id if s.public]`. Splat `[*]` extracts one attribute from a list: `aws_instance.web[*].id`. Nested for + `flatten()` handles multi-level structures. For expressions support both single-value and key-value iterators (`for k, v in map`).

sections.title: Dynamic blocks
sections.body: Generate repeated nested blocks with `dynamic "block_name" { for_each = collection; content { ... } }`. Inside content, access the current element with `block_name.value` (or `block_name.key` for the index/key). Override the iterator name with the `iterator` argument. Supports nesting — dynamic blocks inside dynamic blocks. Use sparingly: overuse reduces readability. Best for security group rules, listener actions, and other highly variable nested blocks.
